arts.ttl: Unai Emery makes 'best team in the world' demand as Aston Villa players sent brutal reminder
arts.sum: Unai Emery insists his Aston Villa players must treat Cardiff City like the best team in the world or they could come a cropper against the Championship side in the FA Cup on Friday night. Unai Emery makes 'best team in the world' demand as Aston Villa players sent brutal reminder

arts.ttl: Rashford up for permanent Villa move - Friday's gossip
arts.sum: Marcus Rashford is prepared to make his Aston Villa loan move permanent, while Liverpool have a growing interest in Alexander Isak.

arts.ttl: Every word Unai Emery said about injuries, FA Cup dreams and possible Cardiff City shock
arts.sum: Unai Emery sat down with the press ahead of Aston Villa's FA Cup fifth round match against Cardiff City Aston Villa manager Unai Emery spoke to the media ahead of the FA Cup fifth round clash against Cardiff City on Friday night.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa supporters deliver Crystal Palace verdicts as Unai Emery questioned
arts.sum: Aston Villa supporters deliver Crystal Palace verdicts as Unai Emery questioned Commenter Mickmark wrote: “I think Emery got it wrong from the off.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa FA Cup fixture vs Cardiff City selected for historic new rule change
arts.sum: Aston Villa will be part of the first use of semi-automated offside technology (SAOT) after the Professional Game Match Officials Board (PGMOL) confirmed the plan to introduce it in the FA Cup this week. The new semi-automated offside technology will be introduced in the FA Cup for the first time this week

arts.ttl: Football ‘paralysis’ claim as Aston Villa fight PSR battle
arts.sum: A Premier League chairman claims the creation of an independent regulator is to blame for many of the game's current ills English football has been "driven into the courts" by the prospect of an independent regulator, a top-flight club chairman has clamed.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa receive new Ollie Watkins offside referee verdict after disallowed goal fury
arts.sum: Former Premier League referee Dermot Gallagher has delivered his verdict on Ollie Watkins' controversial offside in the build-up to Aston Villa's disallowed goal in the first half against Crystal Palace. Aston Villa suffered a 4-1 defeat against Crystal Palace on Tuesday and Unai Emery's side were frustrated when Morgan Rogers' first-half goal was disallowed due to Ollie Watkins being offside

arts.ttl: Tonight's Cardiff City news as Riza finds 'captain material' in quiet leader and Aston Villa sweat on injured trio
arts.sum: The latest Bluebirds news from Cardiff City Stadium

arts.ttl: Matchday Guide: Aston Villa v Cardiff City
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Cardiff City - Fri, Feb 28th 2025, 20:00 The FA Cup rolls into Villa Park on Friday night as Aston Villa vie for a place in the quarter-finals against Cardiff City. Unai Emery is looking to guide the club into the last eight for the first time since a run to the final in 2015. Here's everything you need to know ahead of the encounter to enjoy your visit on matchday... Getting to the game Click on the sections below to learn more about tickets, Villa Park and transport to the stadium. Mobile Tickets Villa Park is now fully NFC ticketing enabled for supporters to access the stadium. Near-Field Communication (NFC) enables fans to enter the stadium via Apple or Google Wallet. Supporters can now also access their match-by-match tickets and enter the stadium directly via the Aston Villa app. Simply log into your Digital Account in the app and your tickets will appear under the ‘Tickets’ section. Supporters must update their app to the latest version to ensure in-app ticketing works without issue. Tickets General admission tickets for the match have sold out, but a limited number of GA+ tickets remain available here. The best way to secure tickets for future matches at Villa Park is via a Membership. Get Matchday Ready Ahead of the match at Villa Park, the club would like to advise supporters of the following ticketing information to ensure everyone enjoys their visit to our stadium. Fans will need to provide one of the two specified ticket types to gain entry to the stadium - access tickets in our official Aston Villa mobile app or download to Apple/Google Wallet for NFC entry. Hospitality and Premium Experiences The club has a range of hospitality and GA+ areas to complement our existing offer. Check out all the information and book online here. Hospitality bookings for our upcoming home matches can be made over the phone on 0333 323 1982 (option 1) or by emailing hospitality@avfc.co.uk. Under-3s Policy Many supporters may not be aware of the policy regarding under-3s, so we are reminding supporters that under-3s are only permitted to be in the Family areas in the Trinity Road stand at Villa Park and must have a match ticket. Further information can be found here Aston Villa Ticketing (avfc.co.uk) Visiting Villa Park It's important that supporters take steps to plan their visit to the stadium. Turnstiles open at 6.30pm, so make sure you arrive at the stadium in plenty of time ahead of kick-off to ensure smooth entry. Please note, access to stiles is maintained during the first half and will close once the half-time whistle is blown. However you're planning on getting to the match, take a look at our Travel & Parking guide before leaving home. A traffic exclusion zone will be in operation around the stadium, meaning there will be less traffic on Trinity Road and Witton Lane from up to two hours before the match starts. The Holte Pub will open three hours prior to kick-off and close two hours after full-time. Key Travel Information Road works are taking place at Trinity Road junction off the A34 Birchfield Road southbound. For more information, please click here. Please plan ahead and check train timetables for direct services or travel earlier for less busy services. Please visit Transport for West Midlands’ Journey Planner or the National Rail journey planner here. Our dedicated city centre shuttle bus service, the AV82, will operate for the match. For more information on the AV82, visit here. Our shuttle bus to and from Tamworth, the AV1, will also be in operation. For more information, visit here. You can also pre-book your ticket for the AV1 bus here. To help you plan your journey by public transport, please visit How to get to Villa Park. Supporters are advised that the supply of car parking around Villa Park is limited. Supporters can pre-book their car parking space via either of the links below. www.astonvillaparking.co.uk www.countrywidemanagementsolutions.co.uk We would strongly encourage all supporters who have not pre-booked their car parking to travel to Villa Park by alternative modes. City Centre and Tamworth Shuttle Buses Aston Villa is delighted to confirm to supporters that our AV82 city centre shuttle bus service will continue to run on matchdays this season. This follows a successful trial of the service at the back end of last season. The service will be in operation for all men’s home matches in the Premier League, UEFA Champions League and all domestic cup competitions, helping supporters travel more easily to and from Villa Park. Run by Stagecoach, the AV82 shuttle picks supporters up from outside Birmingham New Street Station on Dudley Street, running directly to Villa Park. Buses run every 15 minutes, starting three hours before kick-off for every home fixture. Services run back to Birmingham New Street at regular intervals for up to 90 minutes after the final whistle of every game, with services departing from Grosvenor Road, on the walking route towards Aston Station. Traffic signals have been optimised around Villa Park to help improve flow of traffic away from the ground. This should assist with journey times for our AV82 city centre shuttle bus. The club also has a dedicated bus service, AV1, that runs to Villa Park from Tamworth on matchday. You can purchase tickets here. More information is available here. Cycle Parking Covered and secure cycle parking is available a short walk away from Villa Park at our Inner City Academy, Brook Vale, on Witton Road. Accessibility information Supporters with accessibility requirements travelling to Villa Park by rail are advised to look out for the accessibility signage on both platforms at Witton Station. Station staff will be able to help you if you wait in the designated areas. More information is available here. For any supporters with additional mobility needs who have questions about travel to and from Villa Park on a matchday, please contact accessibility@avfc.co.uk. Pre-match activities Click on the sections below to learn more about activities taking place at Villa Park before kick-off. Peter Whittingham Tribute Aston Villa fan group 1897 have organised a banner to be raised before the game in tribute to former Aston Villa and Cardiff City player Peter Whittingham. Designed by 1897, the display will be unfurled as the players walk out of the tunnel and will be seen on the North Stand lower. 1897 have kindly donated the excess money raised from the flag via their GoFundMe to the PW7 Foundation, which was set up by trustees and members of Peter’s family after his passing. Launched in 2021, the Foundation’s mission is to help change the lives of children and young people throughout the UK. They achieve this by raising money for their three chosen charities, empowering young lives and bringing communities together, all while celebrating Peter’s life and creating a legacy in his name. Supporters can donate to the PW7 Foundation here. Enjoy the Fan Zone Villa Park's new Fan Zone is again open on matchday, located in the former Players' Car Park. The new space opens its doors at 5pm, with supporters able to enjoy a bar area and food. The Fan Zone is also in prime position to watch player arrivals from 6.30pm. We also have entertainment taking place on the stage opposite Trinity Reception, including live music from Smokie Coco. We want to see your experience of matchday at Villa Park! Post your photos and tag #UTV and you could be featured on the big screens at the stadium! Supporters are advised that the R44 accessible entrance will be closed for a period of time. Family Fun Zone Work is soon to begin on the transformation of the existing Academy building at Villa Park. Unfortunately, due to the planned works, the Academy building is not operational to the extent to which we can safely open the Family Fun Zone. The club are working on alternate arrangements to enable a continuation of some of the family-friendly pre-match activities. Updates will follow in the coming weeks. Food and Beverage Villa Park offers a wide range of cuisine and beverages on a matchday. Outside the stadium, several street food trucks will sit alongside a bar serving alcoholic and soft drinks from 5pm. Supporters are reminded that Aston Villa adopts a one-cup system at Villa Park, in our continued environmental sustainability efforts. Cashless Stadium Please note, Villa Park will be cashless to help ensure safe social distancing practices and minimise contact at all points of sale. This includes the Villa Store, with card payments widely accepted in and around the stadium. The only exception is programme kiosks outside the ground, which will still accept cash. In-stadia betting kiosks will also be in operation for this fixture and such service will be provided on a cashless basis. North Grounds Update Aston Villa Football Club has announced its plans for a bold regeneration project at Villa Park that will build on the Club’s 150-year heritage, while also creating the fan experience zone of the future. Expected to go live Christmas 2025, the new project includes the launch of The Warehouse, a new multi-use venue set to become a centrepiece of the matchday experience and a hub for year-round entertainment, along with a new ticketing office, improved plaza in the North Grounds, and expanded footprint of the club shop. Admission to The Warehouse will be free for ticketed supporters on matchdays, when the venue becomes the largest beer hall in the Premier League – a vibrant space to connect, enjoy a pint, and soak in pre-match excitement with state-of-the-art LED lights and flat-screen TVs. Beyond matchdays, The Warehouse will transform into the Midlands’ premier concert venue and event space. The 3,500-seater venue will host world-class performances, from live music and sporting events to community activities, creating a versatile space for audiences of all kinds. Designed to bring the Aston and Birmingham communities together, it will also serve as a platform for local and global talent, making an impact far beyond Villa Park. More information here. Matchday Programme The latest edition of the Villa News & Record will be available to purchase at Friday's match! The programme contains a tribute to Peter Whittingham, a feature interview with Mark Delaney and much more. Supporters will be able to purchase copies from sellers at Villa Park on matchday, while they're also available to purchase in the club shop and online here. Villa Store Click on the sections below to learn about merchandise points around the stadium. Store Opening Hours There are a number of places where supporters can shop for Aston Villa merchandise on a matchday, including the upgraded Villa Store. Below you'll find the opening times for each shop around the ground... Villa Store: 9.30am-10.30pm Holte Store: 5–8pm and then 9:30-10:30pm 82 Lounge: 5–8pm and then 8:30-10:30pm Lower Grounds: 5–8pm and then 8:30-10:30pm Trinity Store: 6.30pm until the end of half-time Matchday offer - 50% off selected training wear! Fans visiting Villa Park on Friday will be able to take advantage of an exciting matchday offer of 50% off selected training wear. This deal is redeemable in Villa Park stores on matchday only at the above locations, so make sure you head along to a retail outlet in B6 to make the most of the opportunity. T&Cs apply. During the game Click on the sections below to learn more about events that take place during the match. See it...hear it...report it! If you witness discriminatory behaviour at a match or around a stadium, please report it to the police or a steward. Supporters at Villa Park can text REPORT to 60060 along with the location of the incident and details of what you have witnessed. The text line also includes the reporting of any safeguarding concerns. The text service will be supported by our security team and West Midlands Police. Each text costs one standard message at your network rate. The club may share your details with West Midlands Police if further information is required to assist with a criminal prosecution. All-seater Stadium We would like to remind supporters that Villa Park is an all-seater stadium. In all areas of Villa Park, we have children, elderly fans and those with medical conditions who are unable to see or watch the match if supporters stand up in front of them. Please be mindful of this at all times and thank you for your cooperation. Fan Engagement Click the sections below to learn about how you can get involved on matchday and our fan loyalty programme, Villa Rewards. Get Involved New for 2024/25, the ‘Villa Predictor’ game will give fans the opportunity to win a £500 store voucher if they correctly predict the outcome of the game. T&Cs apply. We want to know about your matchday experience at Villa Park. Tweet us using #UTV and you could feature on the big screens at the stadium! Pride Rewards The Pride Rewards Portal is back up and running. Log in here. AV150 Legacy Wall Aston Villa is excited to reveal a new AV150 Legacy Wall at Villa Park as part of our 150th anniversary celebrations. Supporters have the opportunity to become part of our iconic home, wherever you are in the world, by purchasing your very own personalised plaque at Villa Park. Our Legacy Wall is a place where you, your friends and family can show your support for the club, celebrate births, marriages, anniversaries or pay tribute to loved ones. For more information, click here.

arts.ttl: Emery says Villa 'not contenders' to win FA Cup
arts.sum: Manager Unai Emery says Aston Villa are "not a contender" to win the FA Cup this season.
